scenario_title: Duplicate add_rr of an identical RR in one UPDATE

group_1.behavior: Coalesces identical adds or treats the second identical add as an idempotent no-op, accepting the UPDATE.

group_2.behavior: Processes the two add_rr operations sequentially and treats the second identical add as erroneous once the RR is already present, or otherwise rejects the UPDATE.
group_2.post_state: d.campus.edu. absent or unchanged from pre-update state.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.2 defines add_rr semantics but does not say whether duplicate identical add_rr entries inside a single UPDATE must be merged before application or may be evaluated sequentially. RFC 2181 §5.2 clarifies RRset membership semantics, but not intra-message deduplication. As a result, implementations may differ on whether the second identical add is ignored, accepted as redundant, or rejected after the first creates the RRset member.
expected_divergence.test_rationale: This UPDATE isolates the underspecified case by sending two syntactically valid, identical add_rr operations for the same owner/type/class/rdata in one message. A server that deduplicates or treats repeated identical adds as idempotent should return NOERROR and leave d.campus.edu. with the A record, while a server that applies operations sequentially and checks membership after each add may reject the second operation and leave the post-state unchanged. The follow-up QUERY makes both the reply code and the resulting RRset observable as a binary oracle.
